examples: The show isn't over until the fat lady sings.
The strawberries are over now. I picked the last few yesterday.

definitions.text: Of a flower: wilting or withering.
Describing a physical change of position or state. From one position or location to another, horizontally or approximately so, or along a route visualised as "across".
examples: That vase isn't exactly central. Could you move it over a couple of inches?
I slid over to make room for him to sit down.

definitions.text: From one position or location to another, horizontally or approximately so, or along a route visualised as "across".
examples: That vase isn't exactly central. Could you move it over a couple of inches?
I slid over to make room for him to sit down.

definitions.text: Across from one side of something to the other.
examples: I need to cross the river. Can you take me over in your boat?

definitions.text: A set of six legal balls bowled.
examples: Jones hit four boundaries in the first over.

definitions.text: Any surplus amount of money, goods delivered, etc.
Something having an excess of a particular property.
examples: I went fishing but caught mostly overs.
In today's golf tournament we recorded three unders and twenty overs.

definitions.text: Expressing spatial relationship or movement. Above; higher than; further up than.
examples: Hold the sign up over your head.
The mountain towered over the village.

definitions.text: Above; higher than; further up than.
examples: Hold the sign up over your head.
The mountain towered over the village.

definitions.text: Across, from one side to the other.
examples: The guide took us over the border.

definitions.text: A radio procedure word meaning that the station is finished with its transmission and is expecting a response.
examples: Bravo Six, this is Bravo Six Four. Stand by for ten mike report one dash three, over.
Bravo Six Four, this is Bravo Six Actual. Send your traffic, over.

definitions.text: Instructs the reader to turn the page and continue reading the other side.
Ellipsis of over the left shoulder (“expressing disbelief etc.”).
To go over, or jump over.
examples: He overed the fence in good style.
The cattle have been overing all day because of the flies.
The sea's over.
